oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Management of special pens in beef operations
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Nelson Warr, Brian
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Feedlot special pens include treatment, chronic, railer and rider pens. Treatment pens house animals requiring daily therapy or observation. Chronic pens house animals that have failed to respond to treatment and require more time for re­covery. It is important to assess the welfare of each animal in the chronic pen on a daily basis. Additionally, recording each animal’s weight on a weekly basis can help provide objective information for an evaluation after the animal has been in the pen for 30 days. Rail pens house animals that require early harvest. Buller pens house steers that have been removed from the home pen due to buller syndrome. Managing special pens requires increased attention to ration details, pen maintenance and biocontainment practices. In cow-calf operations special pens primarily focus on calving. A common issue is for the calving area to be used to house sick animals. Veterinarians should be proactive in making plans with cow-calf producers to have special pens for sick animals.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 28-29
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
